title: Level 393
wikitext:
{{Custom Theme|font=Comfortaa|extra=.right-rail-wrapper, .page__main { background-image: radial-gradient(circle, rgb(var(--kauer-blue), 0.05) 50%, transparent 0); background-size: 0.5em 0.5em; background-repeat: repeat; }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:
'''Level 393: "Cryostasis"'''
}} {{Custom Theme|color=220|linkcolor=225|opacity=0.85}} __notoc__



{{Blurbackground|file=Snowparklake.jpg}}
Act I: I'm Dreaming of a White-
{{Alt Class 2|exit=3|environment=4|entity=1|no=393}}
'''Level 393''' is the 394th level of the Backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=snowpath.jpg|caption=Early Morning}} '''Level 393''' is a sprawling suburban park trapped in an eternal winter. The towering trees now only hold glistening icicles; their leaves long since withered. They form a forest casting faint shadows on the snow-covered ground. Some of them are giants rising to incredible heights, and some of them are mere saplings waiting to grow. The shaking of their branches in the icy breeze and the distant, low rings of windchimes are the only ambience here. Mercifully, wind chill is minimal, making it feel, on average, only a couple degrees below freezing.

A layer of snow smothers out the last leaves of an autumn that was forever ago, and the grass beneath it has long since yellowed in wait for a spring that will never come. Still, the only things to visibly disturb the snow’s glistening surface are the footprints of wanderers themselves. Snow follows the topography of the level perfectly, clinging to hills, dips, and even the tops of trees. The only places free of snow are the serene waters of streams and ponds. Their surfaces are almost completely still and like a mirror, waiting to forever trap anything that crosses over in its icy abyss.

Beneath the withered canopies lies a second forest of black iron lamps. Thousands of spires each hold their own glass star, glowing bright enough to solely illuminate this place. Rows of these lamps line concrete paths that snake across the level like a half-finished spiderweb. They stretch up hills as staircases and across water as bridges made of brick. In some places, they widen into roads, and their companions become streetlamps. These roads are much more elusive and typically lead to nowhere.
{{Blurimage|file=snow_bridge_water.jpg|caption=Reflections|align=left}} In this metal forest, benches would be the foliage. They’re found just off the main path, beckoning travelers to rest, but their death-cold touch will ensure most never wake up again. Descending further into the level, signs with nonsense directions begin to sprout from the ground near the paths; it is advised not to follow. Scattered across the level are the remnants of what the park used to be, statues of faces lost to time and pavilions dedicated to nobody, all fossilized in ice.

There is no contact with the outside, and no food to support anyone. Nothing lurks in the shadows but ice, biting with cold instead of jaws. In this place, the wanderer is [[Phenomenon 15|alone]]. The cycle will continue as it always has. Morning after morning and night after night, this winter will never end. But the ice has yet to freeze the sky, so those mornings still come. There might be no sun or moon, but each day passes the same way.

Weather
== Stage 1 == {{Blurimage|file=Level393snowflood.jpg|caption=Approximately 7:15 AM level time}} Whenever a wanderer enters '''Level 393''', all time-keeping devices will invariably begin to run from 5 AM, regardless of the time of entry.It's disorienting to go from poetic description to clinical. - J Clinical is standard for the database. Information is more important than reading flow. - A These devices will be reset after exiting with the addition of any time spent in '''Level 393'''. Because of this, it is theorized that either many copies of the level exist simultaneously or that its geometry is spherical and it experiences day and night similar to the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]].

This stage begins with a clear blue-purple sky and the temperature starts at around -2 Celsius (28 Fahrenheit). Sunrise begins at approximately 7 AM, at which point the temperature begins to climb. Snowfall in this period is light, typically falling below 0.25 centimeters. This is the only stage with freezing rain, which occurs sporadically and is too small to measure. They're just going to forget these numbers. Just tell them it's really cold or the range. - JThe effect is the same regardless. - A Sunrise begins at around 6:45 AM each cycle, making the already faint shadows grow even lighter. Temperatures begin to climb before striking their peak of 2 degrees Celsius (36 degrees Fahrenheit). Across this entire stage, the air smells unusually fresh, despite the wind failing to break 3 kilometers per hour. This stage progressively grows calmer and warmer till approximately noon each day when the next stage begins.

Despite the darkness, visibility is otherwise excellent. It is even easier to see thanks to the lamps being at their brightest during this stage. In some instances, they can get bright enough to hurt a wanderer's eyes if stared at. It is unknown how they adjust their light level. The sky is clear, so light reflection is not massively contributing. Some bodies of water may have their surfaces frozen, but this ice is incredibly fragile and should not be stepped on. == Stage 2 == {{Blurimage|file=pexels-murat-halici-294274529-20000788.jpg|caption=Approximately 1:10 PM level time.|align=left}} Stage 2 begins at 12 AM and reverses the weather in '''Level 393''' towards something harsher. Over the next 7 hours, the temperature falls down to -4 Celsius (25 Fahrenheit).I believe your report neglected to mention or imply any sort of weather changes, please rectify this in the next draft. - A This change in temperature causes small icicles to form on trees and various other structures. The weather grows noticeably worse, with the sky growing overcast and dark clouds forming over the last couple of hours. Additionally, the wind begins to pick up, getting as high as 15 kilometers per hour, whilst also beginning to exhibit the dryness expected of cold weather.This is a minor anomaly described like it's a weather report. Where is your sense of wonder? - JThe archive exists to keep people informed, not for entertainment. - A Precipitation is the only thing unaffected, as snowfall remains at 0.25 centimeters.

As this stage goes on, the lamps dim, even as sunset begins at approximately 5 PM. This decreases visibility in tandem with a light fog that sets in around halfway through this stage. This fog will persist but will only slightly worsen till this stage's conclusion. Beyond changes to the weather, some pathways might freeze and become slick. Back on Earth, a place like this would be a great mystery. A little bit of that specialness dies with each banal sentence typed. - JDo not continue the misuse notation for platitudes. Your "mystery" is our home. - A In addition, Frostbite starts to become a hazard after a few hours.

Given the worsening conditions in '''Level 393''' and the danger of the third stage, it is heavily recommended that all wanderers '''leave before sunset'''. == Stage 3 == {{Blurimage|file=Winterparknight393.jpg|caption=Approximately 7:40 PM level time.}} The third stage is the harshest known. It begins at 7 PM, and from this point on, '''Level 393''' can be considered a dead zone. It begins with light snowfall that builds and builds with 3 centimeters in the first hour and many more to come. Temperature begins to plunge as the earlier storm clouds now cover the sky. Eventually, blizzard conditions set in. Biting winds lash at wanderers while heavy snow threatens to bury any who attempts shelter under benches. Together, the snow, darkness, and ever-growing fog reduce visibility to almost nothing. Frostbite becomes a certainty rather than a risk. Make no mistake, these conditions ''are fatal''. There's your proof standard procedure can get a message across. - A

The severe conditions of '''Level 393''' in this stage make it hard to fully catalogue the exact weather. Progression after 9 PM is hard to ascertain with so few sources, and progression after 12 AM has never been reliably recorded, if at all. This necessarily begs the question of whether there is a fourth stage or a reset to how the weather was back to how it was in the beginning, speculated to begin around 2 AM. Given the large amount of added snow, it is much more likely that it abruptly resets or that potential level copies fall into the void.Wouldn't it be a miracle if somebody found out? - J
EntitiesI already addressed this. - J
'''Level 393''' is affected by [[Phenomenon 15]] and as a result, entity presence is impossible. Wanderers may see silhouettes of hooded figures, and these are to be taken as hallucinations brought on by a minor level property instead of proof of entities.You neglected to mention these guys. - AI trust readers to understand shadowy figures on a P.15 level are hallucinations. - J
Entrances and Exits
== Entrances == {{Blurimage|file=393exitsnowpath.jpg|caption=An exit to Level 461}} * Rare outdoor paths with snow in [[Level 18]] can lead here. * If there is snow on the ground in [[Level 306]], sleeping on a bench could take you here. * Walking blindly into the woods on [[Level 295]] might take you here. * Going through a window displaying wintery exterior in [[Level 450]] will take you here. * Walking through a lightless corridor in [[Level 993]] will lead here. == Exits == * Any noclip performed in '''Level 393''' will send you back to where you came from. (~45% of exits) * Entering a small house-like structure will take you to [[Level 330.1]]. (~3.5% of exits) * Straight paths of missing trees may lead one to [[Level 461]]. (~15% of exits) * Finding a single shred of living plant-life will transport you to [[Level 626.2]]. (~1.5% of exits) * Entering a freestanding exit door will take you to [[Level 772]]. (~10% of exits.) * Death. (~25% of exits)
Open Notes?
Act II: Shadows on the Cavern Wall
== Note 1: == Five hours ago, I woke up in my room in [[Level 399]] and grabbed a prepacked backpack containing winter gear, a notepad, almond water, rations, and my phone. Then I submitted a note of absence to my supervisor and ate breakfast. As soon as I was finished, I jumped out a window and noclipped through the plane of clouds.
Two hours ago, I arrived at [[Level 993]], made contact with the CDO there to supply myself, and plunged deeper until I found the right corridor.
Five minutes ago, I arrived in '''Level 393'''. {{Blurimage|file=393quietruralsnowpark.jpg|align=center|caption=My first moments on 393}} I have to say, I've missed the bite of winter, and I won't miss it for much longer. I'm in a more rural area with lots of trees, which may become safer when windchill becomes a real problem. I haven't had time to do much searching, so I have very little to report. I will continue and will be cataloging my travels as thoroughly as possible.

If this note is ever read, I apologize to my superiors for my unexpected absence. In truth, I've been planning to do this for a while. Assigning me to this project to combat my homesickness just dangles it right in front of me. I expected to seek permission, but with research wrapping up, my clock has been ticking. It's not just that I couldn't come here afterwards either. I could not afford reassignment, just as I feel you could not release this without full documentation of the entire day here.

I promise not to die.

- James Paige == Note 2: == {{Blurimage|file=393bluehoursnow.jpg|caption=The time is currently 7:00 AM|align=center}} Sunrise has begun, and I have unfortunately found very little. Not that I expected to in the most well-documented part. Who am I kidding? I haven't spent time researching yet. I'd like to say I've been looking for a safe spot for stage 3, but that would be a lie as well. The truth is, I've been wandering aimlessly. Every inch of this place looks like a park, but I've come to realize they don't all look like the ''same'' park. I'm no botanist, but I believe some of the plants don't even grow at the same latitudes.

I realize it doesn't matter. [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] parks are big and have many different sections like '''393'''. Kids needed places to run amok, and adults needed relaxing nature walks, perhaps to reminiscence their own childhood. Oh, I know people back home who'd love it here. I know people in 399 who would too. Alas, 4e level, very unsafe.

But after two hours, I'm starting to wonder if I still love it here. When I was a kid, my brother and I would start snowball fights with all the other kids on the block. We'd roll snow for hours to build snowmen and collect interesting icicles. And when I was in college, I'd go on walks with my roommates. Now it feels like they all left this memory behind, and it grew to swallow me whole.

Is it this notepad? Is it the memories? Is it the [[M.E.G.]]? Why does it feel like it's taunting me all of a sudden?
== Note 3: == I think something's following me.

I know that sounds crazy, but I keep seeing something moving out of the corner of my eye. It looks like a figure in a hood, but whenever I turn, it's gone. You'd think it'd be easier with the staunch lack of cover. Now, given [[Phenomenon 15]] on '''393''', I assume this is a hallucination. That doesn't make it not odd that I only saw it now, or that almond water doesn't get rid of it. This thing was in the documentation, so it must be a property of the level. Strange. {{Blurimage|file=snowparkpath.jpg|align=center|caption=My attempts to get it on camera}} Otherwise, at the current time (11 AM roughly), my research has been unsuccessful. I've spent too much time chasing the damn thing. It would be fun if this backpack were not so heavy. In a weird way, it's keeping me company. My only regret is the tug of war between the hunt and seeing the sights.

And this white world is beautiful. It's like everything in here is covered in sparkling diamonds. If only the sun were out! Alas, I find the sky to be overcast time and time again. But no matter, between the lamps and glimmering snow, I have my own stars. I'm all alone out here, sure, but I'm choosing to think of it as my personal place. I can't let the memories get to me when I could be doing much better. == Note 4 == Nighttime is fast approaching now. I've run out of time to dawdle. I've spent most of the last few hours desperately searching for a hiding spot where I could set up my equipment. Turns out, a flat plane of snow doesn't have the best hiding spots. I thought my best chance was with karst topography, but my little shadow friend came to help. {{Blurimage|file=snowforestrock.jpg|align=center|caption=This is what it lead me too}} So it's a rock, which at the best angle gives me 40% directional cover, but it's also in a thick patch of trees near a road, so even more cover. I've got a tent and some equipment I picked up from the [[Level 993 #Colonies_and_Outposts|CDO]] to set up. I've got half of it prepared already, but the time limit is getting to me. Heating pads, a waterproof tent, and good-old-fashioned insulation couldn't compare to the anxiety of time. Three more hours till the blizzard hits. That's the time separating me from certain death. I could back out now... no, I have to see this through. Nobody else can. Who cares if my paradise is crashing down around me? Who cares if I can't grow over the past? Who knows me enough to care if I... I can't think about that either.

Three hours. If I have to worry, let it be about research. The shadow, right? It's weird how it let me here. Is it luck? [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_man_factor Third man syndrome]? Is something here rooting for me? And the giant rock! It's not impossible, but it's unusual for '''393''''s geography. I can spend my time in the tent thinking about these. I'm going to make it through, I'm going to be fine, nobody is going to doubt me anymore.

God, if you're real, drink to this not being the last thing I write. == Note 5 == {{Blurimage|file=snowblizzard.jpg|align=center|caption=Im oit of time,}} ...
...
...
I made it to safety. I took that photo when the snowfall got blizzard levels and sprinted over to my tent. I thought I could stay out a little after 7 and be fine, but it came down fast. I barely had time to turn on all my equipment and zip the entrance up. Now I'm wrapped in a sleeping bag under several blankets, able to see only by the light of this phone alone. And I'm shivering, both from the cold and from the fear that I'll freeze to death, or the tent will blow away, or it'll start hailing. No, it's not fear...

It's more like trepidation.

I've told myself before that I'm going to die here. Whether it be by an entity attack or a dumb mistake like this one. To say I fear it would imply that I'm not already dead. To live for me is to cling to the impossible hope that I'll ever leave the Backrooms. I do not want to die, but only because of a primal survival instinct instead of something to live for. My friends, my family, everyone, and everything I've cared about is gone. And for what? I get to be a clueless nobody working in a toxic environment as if I never even left.

I'm not sure I want to go back anymore once this is over. I hate that I can't be content with this. I hate that I went out that night and ended up here at all. So many wanderers clip into here, and they're all fine. Why am I the exception? Why am I unable to see the upside? == Note 6 == The tent tore.

I heard it while writing the previous note, and I froze. I felt the cold seeping into the tent. I waited, but the dampness of snow never formed. All I could think about was how my stupid obsession was getting me killed. When I checked my phone, it was almost midnight. I should be excited, but I can't be anything but terrified. I found myself the slightest bit relieved. What did it mean to survive, to die, to survive in death? I know I am afraid. I know some part of me clings to this thing devoid of living. I know I'd always wanted to live.

I'm going to wait the storm out. I came in here knowing I might live and with a plan to make the most of it. I'll find a way to make it back.
== Note 7 == The storm is over.

When I was sure the snow had stopped, I checked the time. 2:11 AM. Struggling to believe my eyes, I crawled out of the pile of heavy fabrics I was hiding in. Lamplight and cold air streamed in from a small tear in the tent roof. Instead of a pile, I saw only a few flecks of snow and ice on the floor. I released a sigh I didn't know I was holding; I had done it, hadn't I?

For the first time since I arrived at 393, I smiled. And that smile vanished soon. I had no ability to distinguish between joy and relief in that headspace.

I'm heading outside now. I need to see what has become of the world.

== Note 8 == {{Blurimage|file=393aftertheblizzard.JPG|caption=I can't hear the windchimes anymore|align=center}} I didn't expect it to be this bright. Is it the 40 centimeters of snow or have the lamps grown brighter again? They must've, everywhere I look, I expect to see a city skyline just over the trees. Everything in sight is coated with frost now. It feels less like 393 and more like home, physically frozen in time. The snow will melt soon, though; my thermometer says it's 5 Celsius, and without any wind chill too! It doesn't feel like a memory anymore. I'd never see a storm this bad back home. I suppose the last question is what will happen to me now that time has resumed?

I could go back soon. I wouldn't be in this mess if I had left earlier. At the same time, my job with the archive is soul-draining. But 399 is a big place, so there has to be other stuff out there. It's a little selfish to think of doing that. Maybe I should've seized my life by the reins earlier. I got caught up in missing the outside, and I allowed myself to miss the Backrooms, didn't I? I guess that choice can be made later. I don't want to linger here for much longer, though, that's for sure.

After packing, I became without direction now, prancing between paths like the child I used to be. To say I am without destination implies I need one. I can feel the cold in my body and in the air, but I can almost feel the lamps that seem to warm like sunlight. It's new, it's old. It's the Backrooms, it's the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. I can only fear when this middle ground will erode, and that is no fear at all.

And so I continue forth, over hills, bridges, and dips, navigating under artificial starlight no craftsman has ever touched. I feel my heart rate settle as I draw closer to something.

== Note 9 == Through the trees, I saw a bench at the edge of the pond. When I drew closer, I saw a placard on its back...

Dedicated to James Paige
Act III: Sunrise, Sunset
I dropped my phone in shock. ''It has to be a coincidence.'' I knew James was a common name, but I also knew mere shock wouldn't have made me drop the phone. Something about this felt... significant. None of the other benches had dedications, and it was the only thing of note around here, a bench by the still water looking out. I did find it eerie that the size of it made it so that the other shore was obscured by darkness, but I could still see lamps in the distance.

And in that moment of quiet, I could reflect on how tired I was. Presented with this bench, I was reminded of how tired my legs were. Though I felt entrapped, I let myself fall into the bench. Its cold metal stung my flesh through the jacket, but I was frankly too tired to care. I knew the dangers of sleeping in the cold, but maybe if I just sat for a while...

When I came too, the sky was a light shade of blue, as if in the early morning. ''Oh, I must've been out for hours.'' It certainly felt a few degrees warmer. Groaning, I pushed myself up, shivering from the cold metal of the bench. ''Idiot, you could've died sleeping on cold metal''. I chastise myself as I push off the... makeshift blanket I'm under... ''How the hell?''



"Careful nodding off, sleep for too long and the cold will get you."


I at from the suddenness of the voice. Then I remembered the Alone effect and breathed a sigh of relief. ''Why would an entity or a stranger sound like that?'' I rationalized. At last, I turned, seeing a hooded figure in dark clothing who appeared to be watching me. ''It's no big deal, you're just going a little crazy.'' I unzipped my backpack, took out a canister of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], sipped it, and... the thing remained.

On instinct, I threw the canister only for the strange figure to catch it. I could see its hands were gloved. Its face was obscured by a scarf and the hood, but I could make out the expression in its eyes. Calmness... and pity.

"I suppose you have questions?"

 "How? I should be-"

"This is your own copy of 393. I can only be here because I was created here... sort of.


This made ''some'' sense, if little. This sort of threw a wrench in my ability to guess what this being was. While I was trying to wrap my head around everything, it walked over and sat down next to me on the bench. With this better look, I realized its facial features were my own. Yet it was distinctly another person. ''From another time?'' Then it clicked for me.

"You're a [[Phenomenon 9|tulpae]]! But how are you ''me?''."

"Somehow, you believed in this version of you. If I had to guess, the human imperfection of your conception meant I technically didn't already exist."

I turned away from the figure. I was uneasy thanks to this space's invasion by such an... interesting party. The only thing I could do was ask questions.

"That was you earlier, right?"

"It was."

"Why did you help me?"

"I can only exist so long as you believe in me. That's hard when you're dead."

Its voice... my voice was light and joking. ''So, it doesn't just look like me.'' When I looked at it again, it was smiling warmly, as if at peace.

"Why confront me now and not earlier?"

"You were... not doing the best. You know we don't know the words to help."

I stared at it, waiting for more of an answer.

"I can only manifest here on 393 under specific circumstances."

"And those are? Is there a magnification of psychic energy here?"

"Precisely, and you getting lost in nostalgia was the little boost I needed."

I considered this for a moment, relaxing in the chair. ''But that just raises questions of a different sort.'' I shuddered, from the cold or from dread of that lingering question.
"When I leave here... will you '''die'''?"

The air felt colder suddenly. I instantly regretted the decision when its face went blank. But seconds later, it broke into an amusing, teasing laughter.

"I'm a thoughtform. By nature, I can't be alive or dead. I will lose this body when you leave, sure, but I won't cease to be unless you stop believing."

"If it's any reassurance, I don't think I'll forget this."

There was a silence as I awaited a response. Its face dipped into something uncomfortable and the slightest bit hostile. It quickly vanished into one of apology. It slipped off a glove and showed me its arm. I felt like I was going to vomit when I saw it. A fleshy tumor was snaking its way up its arm. It looked as if skin had crumbled to ash and flaked off. I looked away.

"But you have forgotten."

I wanted to believe he'd be angry, but his voice was hurt and desperate.

"What's wrong with your arm?"

"Your sense of self is changing."

"But if every time I change a tulpae of myself dies, it must have happened many- Are you asking me not to change?"

"The problem is watching you waste ''our'' life. If only I had a body to stop you. But I can't get one without you willingly ceding control."

I burst out laughing. Was I uncomfortable? Was I off guard? I took a moment to collect myself, waiting for it to laugh as well.

"Like that's ever going to happen."

"You came here knowing you might die. Why not?"

No longer could I hope this was anything light. ''It wants to get rid of me...''I was staring a threat in the face, and that threat was myself. It didn't matter if it looked as if it was about to cry; this thing was dangerous.
"Go fuck yourself."

The sorrow vanished from its face as it realized it wasn't working, but I saw that soul-crushing despair lingering in its eyes. It laughed, disturbingly low, almost taunting. It flashed a smile that appeared caring at first, but was so indescribably hurt and wrong. ''Am I really like this?'' It reached out its arm, setting it on my leg.

"Oh come on, what do you have to live for? I get freedom, you get freedom. No more missing the past, no more feeling out of place, no more boring, repetitive days."

"You're evil! Back-"

"I know you, and you hardly consider it a life at all. I could change that, take things right back to how they used to be, where we were happy. Those days might be gone, but we did something right, didn't we?"

"I- I'm not going to let you kill me."

"No! Don't get the wrong idea, you won't die. You'll just take my spot as the tulpae. I'll bring it all back. We can be happy together."

"How could I be happy if you steal my right to my own body?"

"We're the same, are we not? Let me carry the burdens from now on and let yourself reap the rewards."

''He's lying, that makes no sense! But none of this has ever made sense.'' Regardless of if he was lying or not, I knew I had no reason to take the deal. Pretending like the last minute hadn't happened, I pushed its arm away.

"I plan to keep moving forward. I can find something to live for myself."

"Who says you ever will? If it were so easy, why haven't you found one yet? I will leave no ambiguity. I will give us control."


It reached out its rotten, tumorous hand, waiting for me to take the deal. His face was expectant. In it, I recognized myself in a way I felt I shouldn't have.

"What will it be? '''Self-destruction''' or '''salvation'''?"

"I can't allow this."

"What?"
The composure on his face broke, no longer that suave thing he wanted to be. Fear and desperation clouded his gaze, but I still recognized myself. I could only feel pity instead of anger.

"The person you want me to be is dead. I would still be him if life allowed me. If I'm not, he's dead for good reason."

"I'm still here, you can't-"

"I can't live my life pretending I'm still on Earth. That's gone, I can't go back, why bother?"

"Because you were happy, and this clearly isn't working."

 "So I'll try something new, big deal."

"How do you know it'll work? I won't fail us. You trust me, right?"

 "I don't know if it'll..." "So why are we fighting?"

"There was a time in my life when I could live happily as you, but this is a completely different world now. When you're lost, you look for a path, because the one you're on clearly isn't working."

"Please? You're killing yourself! This is what you are; the rest is conjecture."

 "And what if I'm not happy as you? What do you find so wrong about me living again?"

Because I'd die for nothing.

"I think that growth is not a tumor, but a chrysalis. We're going to change together."

"It hurts, you sick fuck. I'd love for you to be right, but I fear-"

 "I'd be lying if I said I wasn't scared, too. You've served me well, and it feels wrong to allow something so ''me'' to die if it is a tumor. But change hurts, and it's the fear of pain that's precisely why I'm here in a slice of a home I'll never see again, freezing my ass off."

"Please... I can't let you do this to yourself."

 "I love you, and you were a wonderful dream. But I have to wake up one day."

I pulled him close, in a last hug. He sobbed, and the tears melted his skin, and the wind blew away his snow. In moments, I was hugging air. ''Please, live on with me''. Shaking, I rose to my feet, turned my back, and walked away from the bench. I didn't intend to look back, but when I heard shuffling, I couldn't resist... {{Blurimage|file=snowbench393goodending.jpeg|caption=... And I saw birds.|align=center}}
== Note 10 == It's been ten minutes.

I don't have much to say.

I originally intended to leave the second I stood up, but those birds stopped me. There ''couldn't'' be birds here; even [[Phenomenon 9|he]] confirmed I was [[Phenomenon 15|otherwise alone]]. I must've run around for a while, searching to see how many more animals there were. I found squirrels scurrying up the frozen trees, birds singing their morning songs, and rabbits hopping across the snow-covered fields.

By the time I was convinced this was real, I sat down on a nearby bench. I sat there, trying to calm myself down as I remembered his words. I knew part of me believed him, but was the part that won fear or wisdom? I decided it didn't matter since what was done was done. I wanted to take joy in my victory, but I prayed we wouldn't be trapped in this cycle, doomed to rot away. Would this make me afraid to move on too? I had to honor him, and learn to keep changing if I ever wanted to not get stuck again.

As I looked back down at the frozen ground, I noticed something green poking through the ice. A small stem, the first fresh bud I had seen. Rationally, I knew it sprouted too early and would wither, but I also knew it would decompose into nutrients for when everything melted. A joy overcame me at the thought. If spring could come here, it felt a little more likely it could come to me. == Note 11 == Five Hours Ago, I survived the blizzard of '''Level 393'''.

Two Hours Ago, I made it 24 hours here, a first on record.

Five Minutes Ago, I saw the end of winter.

See you soon, let this be my two-week notice.
